title: pimelea ciliata subsp. ciliata
intro: Pimelea ciliata subsp. ciliata, also known as hairy rice flower or hairy pimelea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pimelea ciliata subsp. ciliata include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant include making a tea from the leaves and stems, or using the plant in a salve or ointment to apply topically.
